questions.question: Which CSS selector targets elements with a specific class name?
questions.explanation: A period (.) followed by the class name is the CSS class selector. For example, .highlight targets all elements with class="highlight".

questions.question: Which CSS selector targets a single element with a unique ID?
questions.explanation: A hash (#) followed by the ID name is the CSS ID selector. For example, #header targets the element with id="header".

questions.question: What does the descendant selector `div p` target?
choices:
All

elements that are inside a

element

All
and

elements on the page

Only

elements that are direct children of
questions.explanation: A descendant selector (space between selectors) targets all elements matching the second selector that are nested anywhere inside the first selector.

questions.question: Which has the highest specificity?
questions.explanation: ID selectors have the highest specificity among simple selectors. The order from lowest to highest is: universal (*), element, class, ID.

questions.question: In CSS, ___ determines which style rule is applied when multiple rules target the same element.
questions.explanation: Specificity is the algorithm browsers use to decide which CSS rule takes priority when multiple rules apply to the same element.

questions.question: A CSS ___ selector targets elements by their HTML tag name, such as h1 or p.
questions.explanation: An element selector (also called a type selector) matches all instances of a given HTML tag, such as p { } or h1 { }.
